chunk_summary: The PM POSHAN scheme integrates school accountability and food safety through local governance, quality procurement, and hygiene standards to ensure effective pediatric nutrition delivery in schools.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of local bodies and School Management Committees (SMCs) in monitoring PM POSHAN implementation.
Identify key infrastructure and staffing norms critical to meal quality and safety.
Evaluate the impact of procurement standards and hygiene practices on food safety and nutrition outcomes.
key_facts: The PM POSHAN scheme emphasizes local accountability through Gram Panchayats, Municipalities, and SMCs.
Staffing norms for cook-cum-helpers directly influence meal regularity and timely serving.
Procurement standards mandate packed ingredients with quality markings and expiry-date checks.
Mandatory tasting by teachers and periodic tasting by SMC members are required for quality assurance.
Inadequate staffing and poor infrastructure can lead to irregular meal delivery and contamination risks.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Guidelines assign supervision roles to local bodies (such as Gram Panchayat or Municipality where applicable) and position the School Management Committee (SMC) under the Right to Education framework to monitor implementation, oversee meal quality, and ensure cleanliness and hygiene.
document_key_topics: School accountability in PM POSHAN implementation
Role of local governance in pediatric nutrition
